findings.evidence: New pointwise iff lemmas are at lines 111 and 129, but set-level lemmas at lines 123 and 150 are one-way; Mathlib’s parallel value-side API has both `harmonicAt_comp_CLE_iff` and `harmonicOnNhd_comp_CLE_iff` at `Mathlib/Analysis/InnerProductSpace/Harmonic/Basic.lean:198,207`.
findings.fix: Add `harmonicOnNhd_comp_linearIsometryEquiv_iff` and `harmonicOnNhd_comp_add_right_iff` (or change these declarations to iff-style lemmas) deriving the reverse direction via `l.symm`/translation by `-a`, and keep one-way methods as aliases if useful.
findings.issue: The set-level declarations `HarmonicOnNhd.comp_linearIsometryEquiv` and `HarmonicOnNhd.comp_add_right` only prove preservation, despite the pointwise API being iff and the intended statement being invariance.

summary: The pointwise invariance API is bidirectional, but the set-level harmonic API exposes only one-way preservation. Add the missing iff forms so downstream users can use harmonicity on transformed neighborhoods without manually unpacking the definition.
